draft.title: Adequate report on testing
draft.markdown: Automated tests detect regressions early in the development cycle and improve refactoring safety. They form the backbone of CI pipelines and reduce manual QA workload. Teams that invest in tests can ship changes faster with fewer rollbacks. Regression suites should run on every pull request to keep mainline green. Mature teams also track coverage trends over time to detect quality drift. End-to-end tests complement fast unit tests by guarding cross-service contracts. Investing in test fixtures and factories reduces flakiness over time and keeps signal high for engineers.

sections.title: Why tests
sections.content: Tests catch regressions early and let engineers refactor safely. Regression suites form the foundation of CI pipelines and gate every pull request before merge.

sections.title: Trade-offs
sections.content: Maintaining a test suite costs engineering time but reduces production incidents and on-call burden.

sections.title: Best practices
sections.content: Run tests on every pull request and track coverage trends quarterly.
